{"query":"Nocturna horror label Muschietti They Will Kill You release date","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1452,"verdict":null,"extra_info":{"type":"featured_snippet","text":"Hitting theaters March 27, 2026 from Skydance's new horror label Nocturna is They Will Kill You starring Zazie Beetz.Dec 23, 2025"},"results":[{"url":"https://deadline.com/2024/07/new-line-zazie-beetz-muschiettis-they-will-kill-you-1236027717/","title":"New Line To Co-Finance & Release Zazie Beetz Horror Pic ‘They Will Kill You’ From Skydance & Muschiettis’ Nocturna","content":"They Will Kill You follows a woman who answers a help-wanted ad to be a housekeeper in a mysterious New York City high-rise.
Nocturna, which was launched earlier this year by Skydance and the Muschiettis, produces high-quality, horror features for global audiences in both theatrical and streaming."},{"url":"https://skydance.com/news/it-duo-andy-and-barbara-muschietti-skydance-launch-horror-label-nocturna/","title":"‘It’ Duo Andy and Barbara Muschietti, Skydance Launch Horror Label Nocturna - Skydance Media","content":"Andy and Barbara Muschietti, the filmmaking team behind the $1 billion grossing It movies and last year’s The Flash, have teamed up with Skydance to launch a brand new horror division.
The first project out of the gate will be They Will Kill You, written by Kirill Sokolov and Alex Litvak, with Sokolov, who wrote and directed the 2019 black action comedy Why Don’t You Just Die!, attached to direct.
They produced films such as Jeremy Saulnier’s 2018 crime drama Hold the Dark and 2021’s supernatural thriller Séance, written and directed by Simon Barrett.
And the genre, when it hits, can be extremely profitable, especially if there’s a crossover into areas such as merchandizing and theme parks (think Halloween Horror Nights and various activations).
Gary Dauberman, the writer of the It movies, recently signed a first-look at Sony and Screen Gems while Evil Dead Rise filmmaker Lee Cronin this week inked a deal with New Line.
They broke box office records with It and It: Chapter Two, the adaptations of the Stephen King novel, with It scoring the biggest opening weekend ever for a horror film, grossing $701.7M worldwide.
While they are developing a Batman movie called The Brave and the Bold at DC Studios, the duo have not yet committed to their next feature."},{"url":"https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/it-duo-andy-and-barbara-muschietti-1235852829/","title":"'It' Duo Andy and Barbara Muschietti Launch Horror Label Nocturna","content":"Nocturna is not launching with an empty casket.
“Under this label, we are excited to deliver the full range of emotions that our movies pack: Heart, humor and horror,” said the Muschiettis in a statement.
The brother-sister duo made their feature debut with the horror hit Mama.
The Muschiettis are repped by WME and Johnson Shapiro.
Sokolov, whose Russian language Why Don’t You Just Die won the audience award and best new director at Fantasia when it premeired, is repped by CAA, Good Fear, and Morris Yorn."},{"url":"https://www.thewrap.com/new-line-co-finance-distribute-they-will-kill-you/","title":"New Line Set to Co-Finance and Distribute New Horror Movie","content":"New Line is set to co-finance and distribute “They Will Kill You,” a new horror feature from Nocturna Pictues, the label formed earlier this year by Andy and Barbara Muschietti and Skydance.
David Ellison, Dana Goldberg, Don Granger, Andy Muschietti, Barbara Muschietti, Dan Kagan are set to produce “They Will Kill You,” with Russell Ackerman, John Schoenfelder, Sokolov, Litvak and Carl Hampe serving as executive producers."},{"url":"https://www.yahoo.com/entertainment/line-co-finance-distribute-horror-170000770.html","title":"New Line Will Co-Finance and Distribute Horror Movie ‘They Will Kill You’ from the Muschiettis’ Nocturna Pictures","content":"New Line will co-finance and distribute the horror movie "They Will Kill You" from Nocturna Pictures, directed by Kirill Sokolov and starring Zazie Beetz as a woman who takes a job in a mysterious New York City high-rise.
"They Will Kill You," which is directed by Kirill Sokolov from a script by Sokolov and Alex Litvak, stars Zazie Beetz as a woman who answers a help wanted ad for a housekeeper."}]}

Kirill Sokolov will direct from a script by Sokolov and Alex Litvak. *They Will Kill You* follows a woman who answers a help-wanted ad to be a housekeeper in a mysterious New York City high-rise.
